title: Emergency and Disaster Management Program Learning Outcomes
content:
Emergency and Disaster Management Program Learning Outcomes

Program Learning Outcomes

To have basic, up-to-date and practical knowledge about Emergency and Disaster Management.

Have knowledge about occupational health and safety, environmental awareness and quality processes.

Know about emergency communication systems.

Understands how resources are utilized during emergencies and disasters.

Follows current developments and practices in Emergency and Disaster Management and uses them effectively.

Effectively uses information technologies (software, programs, animations, emergency and action plan preparation, etc.) related to the profession.

Ability to independently evaluate professional problems and issues using an analytical and critical approach, and propose solutions.

To be able to prepare scenarios and lead in the field of Emergency and Disaster Management.

Be able to effectively present his or her thoughts through written and oral communication at the level of knowledge and skills, and express them clearly.

Takes responsibility for solving complex and unforeseen problems encountered in applications related to Emergency and Disaster Management.

Has an awareness of career management and lifelong learning.

Include social, scientific, cultural, and ethical values in the stages of data collection, implementation, and announcement of results related to Disaster Management.

Understands minimum standards for disasters and emergencies.

Develops the ability to understand and explain fire safety and firefighting issues.
